description_original.content:
Food policy Trainee (6 months traineeship)

Organisation: BEUC – The European Consumer Organisation
Location: Brussels, Belgium
Engagement: Internship (CIP)
Compensation: €1,325/month (CIP scheme)
Deadline: 26 June 2026
Work arrangement: Hybrid
Source: https://www.beuc.eu/vacancy/food-policy-trainee

## About the role

BEUC – The European Consumer Organisation is looking for a Food policy trainee to support the implementation of BEUC’s strategy on food under the supervision of the team One health – dealing with food, health, and chemicals safety.

## Responsibilities

- Supporting the development of a toolkit for consumer organisations with advocacy materials to advance policy action at national level on plant-based foods.
- Monitoring and analysing EU developments related to food policy.
- Mapping relevant stakeholders and supporting engagement with EU institutions and civil society.
- Gathering and analysing evidence from BEUC members, academic sources, and other relevant materials.
- Supporting the preparation of meetings and events, including drafting agendas, background notes, and follow-up actions.
- Taking minutes of internal and external meetings.

## Candidate profile

- University degree in public health, law, political science or a related field.
- A strong interest in EU food policy and on initiatives that can help promote plant-based foods and diets.
- Good understanding of EU institutions and decision-making processes.
- Strong analytical skills and the ability to synthesise complex information.
- Very good drafting skills in English.
- Good organisational skills and attention to detail.
- Proactive attitude and willingness to learn.
- Previous experience or academic work related to food policy or consumer protection is an asset.

## What BEUC offers

- A 6-month paid traineeship under a Convention d’Immersion Professionnelle (CIP) with a competitive package including:
  - An allocation of 1,325€ per month.
  - Fringe benefits: Homeworking allowance, public transport, 12 days extra-legal paid leave.
  - Flexitime, homeworking possibility (up to 3 days per week), work-life balance.
- A welcoming onboarding.
- A workplace committed to support staff growth and well-being.
- A multidisciplinary and multicultural working environment within an organisation dedicated to promoting consumer interests.

## Location and working arrangements

As the contract is subject to Belgian law, candidates must be committed to official registration in Belgium and working from BEUC’s Brussels office. Teleworking from home or abroad is possible, subject to the remote-working policy and organisational needs.

## Application process

Applications should be sent by 26 June 2026 by e-mail to apply@beuc.eu with the subject line: Food policy Trainee.

Include:
1. A curriculum vitae, with no photo
2. A motivation letter, maximum one page

Entry into function expected as of 13 July 2026. Interviews as of 1 July 2026 with Head of Human Resources and Head of One Health.

BEUC is an equal opportunities employer.
